currentUse: Protestant church — Greater Generations Tabernacle, 214 Ames St, Brockton MA 02302
summary: St. Casimir's church building at 214 Ames St, Brockton still stands and is in active religious use: business and church directories list Greater Generations Tabernacle (508-587-8510) at the address, and Global True Lithuania reports the building was acquired by a Black-majority congregation for roughly $1 million after the 2008 closure, well below its reported $3 million valuation. The main church, built in 1957 over an older basement church, sits between Ames Street and St. Casimir Avenue in what was once Brockton's "Lithuanian Village." The Lithuanian freedom memorial erected beside the church around 1990 still stands but is damaged and no longer flies the Lithuanian flag; the community removed its metal sword and incorporated it into a new memorial at St. Michael Church in Avon, and built a second protected memorial at the convent cemetery in Brockton, since Massachusetts law prevents demolition of cemetery structures. Lithuanian sacred objects from St. Casimir — including a stained glass window and sculptures — were transferred to St. Michael in Avon, the successor parish, while pews were donated to a church in Tanzania. The successor parish, St. Michael in Avon, remains open with an administrator (Rev. Robert J. Cullen) rather than a pastor, operating in a collaborative with a Saint Joseph parish, with English-only Masses and no Lithuanian liturgy. The Greater Brockton Lithuanian-American community was still holding an annual Lithuanian Heritage Day at St. Michael Avon as of March 2020, organized by John Drusinskas.

developments.headline: Final Mass closes 110-year-old St. Casimir; one of six Brockton parishes closed or merged
developments.detail: The Boston Globe reported that St. Casimir, a 110-year-old Lithuanian parish, held its final service in late June 2008 as part of the Archdiocese of Boston's reconfiguration. Parishioners placed makeshift crosses with written pleas on the church grounds trying to save the parish, long the center of Brockton's Lithuanian Village. Over six years, six Brockton parishes closed or merged, making the city one of the hardest hit in the archdiocese; St. Casimir's school was merged into Trinity Catholic Academy.

sources.title: Brockton's Catholic community squeezed but unshaken by church closings

developments.headline: Building sold to a Black-majority congregation for ~$1 million; now Greater Generations Tabernacle
developments.detail: Global True Lithuania reports the church building at 214 Ames St is now owned by a Black-majority church that acquired it for approximately $1 million, though the property was valued at about $3 million. Current business/church directories list Greater Generations Tabernacle at 214 Ames St, Brockton, MA 02302 (508-587-8510, website greatertab.org). Sale year, deed price, and buyer entity name were not confirmed in any newspaper or registry source opened.

sources.title: Brockton, Massachusetts: Lithuanians and Lithuanian heritage
Greater Generations Tabernacle on Ames St in Brockton, MA

developments.headline: Lithuanian freedom memorial partly dismantled to save it; artifacts moved to Avon and to a protected cemetery site
developments.detail: The red-white obelisk memorial to those who died for Lithuanian freedom, erected beside the church soon after Lithuania's 1990 independence restoration, still stands on the sold property but is damaged and its flagpole no longer flies the Lithuanian flag. Fearing destruction, community members removed the metal sword and incorporated it into a new memorial at St. Michael Church in Avon, and erected another memorial at the St. Casimir convent cemetery in Brockton because Massachusetts law bars demolition of structures in cemeteries. St. Michael in Avon also received a stained glass window and sculptures from St. Casimir; pews were donated to a new church in Tanzania.

sources.title: Brockton, Massachusetts: Lithuanians and Lithuanian heritage

developments.headline: Lithuanian Heritage Day still celebrated at successor parish St. Michael, Avon
developments.detail: The City of Brockton events calendar listed a Lithuanian Heritage Day on March 7, 2020 at St. Michael Church, 87 N. Main St, Avon — Mass at 4:00 PM followed by dinner and a cultural program in the church hall ($15/person), celebrating the culture, history and traditions of Lithuanian-Americans in the Greater Brockton area, organized by John Drusinskas. No later editions were found in searches covering 2023-2025.

developments.headline: Successor parish St. Michael Avon open but reduced: administrator, collaborative structure, no Lithuanian Mass
developments.detail: The Archdiocese of Boston directory (Boston Pilot) lists St. Michael, Avon with an administrator, Rev. Robert J. Cullen, rather than a pastor, operating in a collaborative with a Saint Joseph parish. Masses are Saturday 4:00 PM, Sunday 9:00 AM, and weekday 9:00 AM (Mon, Tue, Fri), all in English — no Lithuanian liturgy is listed. The administrator-plus-collaborative structure indicates ongoing consolidation exposure for the parish that absorbed St. Casimir's community.

sources.title: Saluting the Lithuanians of Brockton, Massachusetts

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions. The archive record (closed June 2008, merged into St. Michael Avon) is confirmed by the Boston Globe (final service late June 2008) and by Global True Lithuania (parishioners directed to St. Michael Avon). The archive note of a "favorable post-closure asset split" is refined, not contradicted: Global True Lithuania documents that a stained glass window, sculptures, and the memorial's sword went to the Lithuanian community at St. Michael Avon, and a protected memorial was built at the convent cemetery. One caution for the record: search engines repeatedly conflate this parish with St. Casimir in WORCESTER, MA (Diocese of Worcester, closed 2008/final Mass 2010, sold February 2011 to Church of the Pentecost USA for $650,000) — those facts belong to the Worcester parish and were deliberately excluded from this Brockton record.
gaps: The sale of the Brockton building (buyer entity name, exact price, and year) could not be verified in a newspaper, diocesan announcement, or registry-of-deeds source; the ~$1 million figure (vs. ~$3 million valuation) rests solely on Global True Lithuania, an enthusiast heritage site, and the Brockton assessor/Plymouth County Registry databases were not directly queryable via fetch (LoopNet parcel page for 214 Ames St returned 403). Whether Greater Generations Tabernacle is the original purchaser or a later occupant is unknown. Current activity level of the Tabernacle congregation as of 2026 was not confirmed beyond directory listings and an existing Facebook page. No Lithuanian Heritage Day listings after March 2020 were found (searches for 2023-2025), so it is unknown whether the annual event survived the pandemic. No preservation campaign, demolition threat, or recent local press about the building was found. The Boston Pilot parish directory page for St. Michael Avon is undated, so the pastor/schedule may not reflect mid-2026.
